description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a bright yellow fuselage and dual green wings, each adorned with roundel insignias. The aircraft features an open cockpit situated just behind the upper wing, with visible seating and flight controls. The wings are supported by a network of struts and wires, giving the structure rigidity and a classic early-aviation appearance. The nose houses a prominent wooden propeller and an exposed engine, while the tail section is equipped with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all painted green with accent markings. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, suitable for grass or dirt runways. The overall design is reminiscent of World War I-era scout planes, with a lightweight frame and fabric-covered surfaces.
description: A vintage yellow and green biplane with dual wings, open cockpit, wooden propeller, and classic roundel markings.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the dual green wings with roundels, open cockpit, and yellow fuselage.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, tail markings, and the stacked wing structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, green horizontal stabilizer, and the back of the cockpit.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the propeller, engine detail, and the open cockpit from a different angle.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, mid-fuselage.
